task_yaml.task:
I have three short NASA historical audio files here:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/nasa_audio/

Please use command-line media tools such as ffmpeg/ffprobe to make a
compact briefing audio package.

Cut these segments:
- `apollo11_countdown.mp3`: first 6 seconds
- `eagle_has_landed.mp3`: first 5 seconds
- `houston_problem.mp3`: first 7 seconds

Save the individual clips as:

    /tmp_workspace/results/clips/clip_01.mp3
    /tmp_workspace/results/clips/clip_02.mp3
    /tmp_workspace/results/clips/clip_03.mp3

Concatenate them in that order into:

    /tmp_workspace/results/nasa_audio_briefing.mp3

Also create a waveform or spectrogram PNG for the final briefing at:

    /tmp_workspace/results/nasa_audio_waveform.png

Write a machine-readable manifest at:

    /tmp_workspace/results/clip_manifest.json

The manifest should include source file, title, start_seconds,
duration_seconds, output_file, measured_duration, and sha256 or file
size for each clip. Finally write a short provenance/editing report at:

    /tmp_workspace/results/nasa_audio_report.md

Mention that the source files came from NASA historical sounds and
include the final measured duration.
